fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 65 is inverted: clamping is applied when `clamp` is False and skipped when `clamp` is True. It should be `if clamp:` instead of `if not clamp:`.

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the clamping block should execute, but the inverted condition `if not clamp` means it is skipped, so the result is unclamped when it should be clamped.
The then-branch of `if not clamp` should only execute when clamp is False; if clamp is True and we are inside the branch, the condition was inverted.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__4 >= min(y0, y1) and y__4 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp is True the final y value must be within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; if clamping is incorrectly skipped this will be violated on extrapolated inputs.
lo__1 is computed and used only inside the `if not clamp` block; if clamp is True, execution should never reach this point.
